title: After-Action Report — app-wide mobile overflow pass
scope: The user runs O27 mostly on a phone and reported "pervasive" overflow issues, attaching a screenshot of the Build a Universe page (/universe/new). That page was the trigger, but the brief was app-wide, so this turned into a full audit of all ~50 templates (fanned out across three reviewers) for real phone-overflow risks — fixed-width controls, non-wrapping flex rows, rigid pixel grids — on top of the universe-builder fix.

rationale.item: On the league-config cards the Home region, Park geometry and Power Play selects were squeezed into a 2-up grid until their option text truncated mid-word — "Americas Pr…", "(varied — all…" — and the Home region label collided with its "🎨 Blend…" button.

universe_new.html is the app's only Tailwind-CDN page; every other template rides on the Bootstrap + design-system CSS in base.html, which already carries a comprehensive set of phone guards. The universe builder is an island those rules don't reach.

base.html still carried a @media (max-width: 768px) block that stacked a <table id="league-table"> into labelled cards — but the universe builder had since been rewritten from that table into a Tailwind .lg-card stack. So the page had no working mobile layout. (Same stranded-feature shape the CLAUDE.md scorecard story warns about, in the other direction — CSS left behind after the markup it targeted was replaced.)

actions.description: universe_new.html — added a phone rule (max-width: 639px, below Tailwind's sm) scoped to #uni-builder .lg-body: Teams and Divisions stay paired on the first row, every field from the third on takes the full row.
Deleted the dead #league-table stacking block (37 lines targeting an element removed when the builder was rewritten) and left a pointer note.

Filter bars — on phones each control now stacks full-width (flex: 1 1 100% + width: 100% !important); lands app-wide: transactions, free agents, stats browser, standings, schedule, leaders, financials, analytics, distributions — anything using .filter-bar.
Safety net — .form-select, .form-control { max-width: 100% } at ≤575px so any stray fixed-width control in a non-wrapping row can't push the page sideways.
Spray-chart panels — drop the inline min-width:340px floor on the game page below 575px.

Added flex-wrap to header button rows on auction.html, pro_worldcup.html, league.html, youth.html, team.html, college_prospects.html, game_scorecard.html, pro_worldcup_team.html, and the signing-round form on free_agents.html.
Rigid rating-slider grids — o27i_player.html and college_prospect_player.html used grid-template-columns: 160px 1fr 88px; shrunk both to 92px 1fr 52px below 575px.
Follow-up fix: freezeLabelCols now only pins when a real non-numeric identity column actually exists among the first three cells (foundText guard); all-numeric stat strips freeze nothing.
Follow-up fix: set --bs-table-color: var(--text) on .table so every body cell follows the theme token — one line, fixes all Bootstrap tables app-wide in dark mode.

risk_posture: the fixes below are surgical, not a rewrite
All new rules are gated at ≤575/639px, and the per-page `flex-wrap` additions are no-ops on desktop (the rows already fit one line there), so desktop layout is unchanged.
CSS / markup-only template edits (no engine, route, or data path touched).

validation_claims.claim: Flask is absent from this sandbox (expected per CLAUDE.md), so this was not exercised against a running server — the reasoning rests on the grid/breakpoint math, the confirmed fact that universe_new.html is the sole Tailwind page, and a brace-balanced check of the base.html stylesheet.
validation_claims.check_method: Inspect templates/base.html for the described rules and run the app to view pages at phone widths

non_actions.item: Not verified on a real device
non_actions.reason: would benefit from a phone pass once the branch is deployed

non_actions.item: Did not touch the dozens of d-flex rows that already carry flex-wrap, the Bootstrap col-md-* grids (which stack below md by default), or anything inside the auto-scrolling table wrappers
non_actions.reason: those were verified sound, not ignored

residual_risks: If a specific page still overflows on-device, a screenshot will pin it down faster than another blind sweep.
open_hooks: Phone pass on a real device once the branch is deployed
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — app-wide mobile overflow pass
Audit finding: the foundation is mostly sound
Follow-up: frozen-column overlap on all-numeric stat tables
Follow-up: dark-mode table body text nearly invisible
unmapped_content: Audit finding details: base.html already carries a solid responsive layer — a global JS pass auto-wraps every <table> in a horizontal-scroll container with frozen label columns; body { overflow-x: hidden } + overflow-wrap: break-word at ≤768px; .filter-bar, .nav-tabs, .hero, .stat-tiles already had phone rules; most inline widths are max-width: (safe). The genuine gaps were: (1) the universe builder, the app's only Tailwind page; (2) filter-bar controls that wrapped but kept cramped inline desktop widths; (3) a handful of non-wrapping action-button groups and two rigid pixel grids. | Follow-up frozen-column detail: freezeLabelCols froze "through the first non-numeric column, capped at 3" — but on a table whose leading columns are all numeric (the player page's Advanced/Standard Batting stat strips, first col BAVG), it never found a text column and pinned the first 3 stat columns instead. Those sticky cells then stuck over the scrolling columns and overlapped — only visible on a tinted/highlighted row, which is exactly how the user spotted it. | Dark-mode detail: .table thead th sets its own color: var(--text), but body cells take Bootstrap's --bs-table-color, which base.html never overrode, so it fell back to Bootstrap's default --bs-body-color (~#212529). | Branch: claude/mobile-responsiveness-overflow-5dumJ.
parser_notes: Clear AAR; includes two follow-up bug-fix sections appended after the main pass. No explicit delegation decisions recorded.
